title: UX polish: layout persistence, splitter bounds, error boundary, keyboard shortcut help, wiring gaps
analysis:
This task covers 10 independent UX polish items across the GUI crate. The codebase uses SolidJS with vitest+@solidjs/testing-library for testing. Key patterns: signals for state, createEffect for side effects, Tauri IPC via typed bridge layer, mock-heavy tests with vi.mock(). Viewport mocks Three.js entirely. App.test.tsx uses module-level vi.mock() for Viewport/Editor/bridge and a capturedViewportProps pattern to verify prop wiring.

Current state analysis:
- (A-9) Panel widths/heights are SolidJS signals (lines 54-56 of App.tsx) with hardcoded defaults (300/300/200), never persisted to localStorage.
- (U4) Resize handlers (lines 262-272) use Math.max(MIN_PANEL_WIDTH, ...) but no upper bound — panels can be dragged wider than the container.
- (X-8) No ErrorBoundary anywhere. App has manual error state for init failures but no catch for render crashes.
- (K1) onOpen callback in useKeyboardShortcuts call (line 95-98) is an empty stub comment.
- (K4) Only 3 shortcuts exist (Ctrl+O, F5, Ctrl+E), no help UI or discoverability.
- (A-7/T-4) handleFitToView (line 228-230) is a stub comment. Toolbar 'Fit to View' button calls it but nothing happens.
- (U8) Viewport accepts onHover prop but App never passes it (line 339-345), so viewport hover events don't update selectionStore.
- (U9) evalStatus not passed to Viewport, making the spinner overlay (Viewport.tsx lines 156-174) dead code.
- (V-12) Grid/axes created in scene.ts (lines 61-65) with no toggle mechanism — always visible.
- (V-14) Tooltip has hardcoded position top:8px, left:8px (Viewport.tsx lines 139-140) — doesn't follow cursor.

Approach: Implement each fix as an independent test→impl pair. Create useLayoutPersistence utility for localStorage abstraction. Create KeyboardHelp overlay component. Extend scene.ts SceneContext to return helper refs. Use existing flyToEntityRef pattern for fitToViewRef. Add pickOpenPath to bridge.ts following pickSavePath pattern. Track pointer position for cursor-following tooltip.

prerequisites.description: Create empty gui/src/hooks/useLayoutPersistence.ts with PanelLayout type export and STORAGE_KEY constant to enable test-first development
Create empty gui/src/components/KeyboardHelp.tsx skeleton (export function KeyboardHelp) and gui/src/components/KeyboardHelp.module.css to enable test-first development

steps.description: Write failing tests for useLayoutPersistence in gui/src/__tests__/useLayoutPersistence.test.ts: (1) loadPanelLayout returns null when no data in localStorage, (2) loadPanelLayout returns parsed PanelLayout when valid JSON exists at STORAGE_KEY, (3) savePanelLayout writes serialized layout to localStorage at STORAGE_KEY, (4) loadPanelLayout returns null (does not throw) when localStorage contains corrupted/invalid JSON, (5) loadPanelLayout returns null when stored object is missing required fields

Implement useLayoutPersistence.ts: export STORAGE_KEY='reify-panel-layout', export type PanelLayout = { editorWidth: number; sideWidth: number; propertyHeight: number }. loadPanelLayout(): reads from localStorage, parses JSON, validates all 3 fields are numbers, returns PanelLayout or null. savePanelLayout(layout: PanelLayout): serializes to localStorage. Both wrapped in try/catch for safety.

Write failing tests in gui/src/__tests__/App.test.tsx (new describe 'App layout persistence'): (1) panel widths initialize from localStorage when valid data exists (seed localStorage before render, verify grid-template-columns reflects stored values), (2) missing localStorage falls back to defaults (300/300/200), (3) resizing left splitter writes updated layout to localStorage (drag splitter, check localStorage value)

In App.tsx: import loadPanelLayout/savePanelLayout from hooks/useLayoutPersistence. Initialize signals from loadPanelLayout() falling back to DEFAULT_* constants. Add createEffect that calls savePanelLayout({ editorWidth: editorWidth(), sideWidth: sideWidth(), propertyHeight: propertyHeight() }) — debounced via setTimeout/clearTimeout (300ms) to avoid thrashing during drag.

Write failing tests in gui/src/__tests__/App.test.tsx (new describe 'App splitter max bounds'): (1) dragging left splitter far right clamps editor width so viewport and side panel remain visible — after a huge rightward drag, editorWidth should be <= containerWidth - sideWidth - MIN_PANEL_WIDTH - 8 (splitter widths), (2) dragging right splitter far left clamps side panel width similarly, (3) dragging side-panel splitter downward clamps property height so constraint panel remains visible

In App.tsx: add a ref to the .main grid div (let mainRef: HTMLDivElement). In handleLeftResize: add Math.min upper bound using mainRef.clientWidth - sideWidth() - MIN_PANEL_WIDTH - 8. In handleRightResize: add Math.min upper bound using mainRef.clientWidth - editorWidth() - MIN_PANEL_WIDTH - 8. In handleSideResize: add ref to side panel div, clamp using its clientHeight - MIN_PANEL_HEIGHT - 4. Fallback to large number if refs not yet set.

Write failing tests in gui/src/__tests__/ErrorBoundary.test.tsx: (1) ErrorBoundary wrapping a throwing component catches the error and shows fallback UI with data-testid='error-boundary-fallback' containing the error message, (2) ErrorBoundary renders children normally when no error occurs, (3) fallback shows a 'Reload' button

In index.tsx: import ErrorBoundary from solid-js, wrap in

Something went wrong

{err.message}

}>. Use inline styles consistent with theme (background, text colors).

Write failing tests in gui/src/__tests__/App.test.tsx (new describe 'App viewport prop wiring'): (1) capturedViewportProps.onHover is a function — calling it with 'entity/path' results in selectionStore.hoveredEntity being updated (verify via PropertyEditor or other downstream prop), (2) capturedViewportProps.evalStatus is defined and reflects engine store state
In App.tsx Viewport JSX (lines 339-345): add onHover={(path) => selectionStore.hoverEntity(path)} and evalStatus={engineStore.state.evalStatus} props to the Viewport component

Write failing tests: (1) In Viewport.test.tsx: Viewport calls fitToViewRef with a function on mount, that function delegates to selection.fitToView. (2) In App.test.tsx (new describe 'App fit-to-view wiring'): capturedViewportProps.fitToViewRef is defined, and after setting it up, Toolbar's 'Fit to View' click triggers the viewport's fitToView function

In Viewport.tsx: add fitToViewRef?: (fn: () => void) => void to ViewportProps, call props.fitToViewRef?.(() => selection.fitToView()) in onMount alongside flyToEntityRef. In App.tsx: add let fitToViewFn: (() => void) | undefined, pass fitToViewRef={(fn) => { fitToViewFn = fn }} to Viewport, update handleFitToView to call fitToViewFn?.()

Write failing tests: (1) In bridge.test.ts: pickOpenPath calls Tauri dialog open() and returns selected path string. (2) In App.test.tsx (extend existing or new describe 'App Ctrl+O open file'): dispatching Ctrl+O triggers file dialog open flow — verify bridge.pickOpenPath is called, then bridge.openFile with returned path

In bridge.ts: import { open } from '@tauri-apps/plugin-dialog', export async function pickOpenPath(): Promise<string | null> that calls open({ filters: [{ name: 'Reify files', extensions: ['ri'] }] }) and returns string path or null. In App.tsx: update onOpen callback to async function that calls pickOpenPath(), if path returned calls bridgeOpenFile(path), then editorStore.openFile(fileData). Wrap in try/catch with toast error.

Write failing tests in gui/src/__tests__/useKeyboardShortcuts.test.ts: (1) dispatching ? key (Shift+/) on document calls onHelp callback, (2) dispatching ? when target is an <input> does NOT call onHelp, (3) dispatching ? with Ctrl held does NOT call onHelp (avoid conflict with browser shortcuts)

In useKeyboardShortcuts.ts: add onHelp?: () => void to KeyboardShortcutCallbacks interface. Add handler in handleKeyDown: if e.key === '?' and !e.ctrlKey and !e.altKey, call callbacks.onHelp?.(). Place it after modifier-key checks to avoid conflicts.

Write failing tests in gui/src/__tests__/KeyboardHelp.test.tsx: (1) renders overlay with data-testid='keyboard-help' containing shortcut list including Ctrl+O, F5, Ctrl+E, ?, (2) clicking close button calls onClose callback, (3) pressing Escape key calls onClose, (4) lists correct descriptions for each shortcut

Implement KeyboardHelp.tsx: export interface KeyboardHelpProps { onClose: () => void }. Render modal overlay (backdrop + centered card) with data-testid='keyboard-help'. Display shortcut table: Ctrl+O → Open file, F5 → Re-evaluate, Ctrl+E → Export, ? → Toggle this help. Close button and Escape keydown handler calling props.onClose(). Style with KeyboardHelp.module.css using theme CSS variables (--reify-surface, --reify-text, --reify-border, --reify-accent).

Write failing test in gui/src/__tests__/App.test.tsx (new describe 'App keyboard help overlay'): (1) pressing ? key shows keyboard help overlay (data-testid='keyboard-help' present), (2) pressing ? again hides it (toggle behavior), (3) pressing Escape while help is shown hides it

In App.tsx: import KeyboardHelp, add const [showHelp, setShowHelp] = createSignal(false). Pass onHelp: () => setShowHelp(v => !v) to useKeyboardShortcuts. Add <Show when={showHelp()}><KeyboardHelp onClose={() => setShowHelp(false)} /></Show> in the JSX (outside the initPhase ready block, so it works in all states).

Write failing tests in gui/src/__tests__/viewport/scene.test.ts: (1) createScene returns grid property that is a GridHelper instance, (2) createScene returns axes property that is an AxesHelper instance, (3) both grid and axes have a visible property (initially true)

In scene.ts: extend SceneContext interface to include grid: GridHelper and axes: AxesHelper. Store the grid and axes in local variables (already created but unnamed), return them in the SceneContext object: { scene, camera, renderer, resize, adjustClipping, grid, axes }.

Write failing tests in gui/src/__tests__/viewport/Viewport.test.tsx: (1) renders a grid toggle button with data-testid='toggle-grid', (2) clicking the toggle button updates grid/axes visible state (mock createScene to return objects with visible property, verify .visible toggled after click)

In Viewport.tsx: destructure { grid, axes } from createScene result. Add const [showGrid, setShowGrid] = createSignal(true). Add createEffect that sets grid.visible = showGrid() and axes.visible = showGrid() and calls requestRender(). Add toggle button in overlay area near fit-to-view button (bottom-right, offset left of Fit button): data-testid='toggle-grid', onClick={() => setShowGrid(v => !v)}, text 'Grid' with visual indicator of state.

Write failing tests in gui/src/__tests__/viewport/Viewport.test.tsx: (1) tooltip style top/left update on mousemove within container (simulate mousemove, verify tooltip has updated position style near the pointer coordinates with offset), (2) tooltip is not positioned at fixed 8px/8px after a mousemove event, (3) tooltip still shows the hoveredEntity text content

In Viewport.tsx: add const [pointerPos, setPointerPos] = createSignal({ x: 8, y: 8 }). Add onMouseMove handler to container div that computes position relative to container (e.clientX - containerRef.getBoundingClientRect().left, e.clientY - containerRef.getBoundingClientRect().top). Update tooltip style to use top: `${pointerPos().y + 16}px` and left: `${pointerPos().x + 16}px` instead of hardcoded 8px values. Keep pointer-events: none on tooltip.

design_decisions.decision: Create useLayoutPersistence as a pure function module (loadPanelLayout/savePanelLayout) rather than a SolidJS reactive hook
design_decisions.rationale: Pure functions are easier to test without SolidJS reactive context (no createRoot needed). The persistence logic is simple read/write to localStorage. App.tsx handles the reactive wiring (createEffect for save, signal init for load). This keeps the module framework-agnostic.

design_decisions.decision: Use flyToEntityRef callback pattern for fitToViewRef — Viewport passes out a closure that App stores as a mutable ref
design_decisions.rationale: This is the established pattern in the codebase (flyToEntityRef at App.tsx line 73 and line 344). Consistent and proven approach for child→parent function exposure in SolidJS where parent needs to imperatively call a child function.

design_decisions.decision: Return grid and axes objects from createScene in SceneContext rather than using scene.getObjectByName()
design_decisions.rationale: Type-safe access without string lookups. Extends SceneContext interface cleanly. The grid/axes are already created in createScene, so returning them is a minimal change. Downstream code gets typed GridHelper/AxesHelper references.

design_decisions.decision: Single grid toggle button that controls both GridHelper and AxesHelper visibility together
design_decisions.rationale: Simplest UI for a polish item. Grid and axes are conceptually related — both are reference helpers for spatial orientation. A single toggle reduces UI clutter. Can be split into separate toggles later if users request it.

design_decisions.decision: Track pointer position relative to container using getBoundingClientRect() on each mousemove
design_decisions.rationale: Tooltip is positioned absolute within the viewport container, so container-relative coordinates are needed. Using getBoundingClientRect() on each move handles cases where the container shifts (e.g. splitter resize). The overhead is negligible since mousemove already fires frequently.

design_decisions.decision: Add pickOpenPath to bridge.ts using @tauri-apps/plugin-dialog open() function
design_decisions.rationale: Follows existing pickSavePath pattern in bridge.ts (line 63-74). Keeps Tauri dialog API usage centralized in the bridge layer. The package @tauri-apps/plugin-dialog is already a dependency (package.json line 29). The open() function is the counterpart to save() already imported.

design_decisions.decision: Debounce savePanelLayout in createEffect with 300ms timeout rather than saving synchronously
design_decisions.rationale: During splitter drag, the resize handler fires on every mousemove (potentially 60+ times per second). Writing to localStorage on every frame would be wasteful and could cause jank. A 300ms debounce captures the final resting position after drag completes.

design_decisions.decision: ErrorBoundary wraps App in index.tsx (entry point) rather than inside App component
design_decisions.rationale: Catches errors from App's own render, not just its children. If App itself throws during initialization or rendering, the boundary still catches it. This is the standard pattern for top-level error boundaries in SolidJS.

design_decisions.decision: Max-width clamping reads ref.clientWidth at resize time rather than maintaining a reactive signal for container dimensions
design_decisions.rationale: Container size changes are infrequent relative to splitter drags. Reading clientWidth directly in the resize handler is simpler, always current, and avoids adding another ResizeObserver or signal sync. The value is only needed at resize-handler invocation time, not reactively.

design_decisions.decision: Keyboard help overlay toggles on ? key and can be dismissed with Escape, placed outside initPhase guard
design_decisions.rationale: Help should be accessible even during loading/error states since the user might need to know keyboard shortcuts before the app is fully initialized. Using ? as the toggle key is conventional (GitHub, Gmail use same pattern). Escape dismissal is expected modal behavior.

reuse.how: Clone the same pattern for fitToViewRef: Viewport calls props.fitToViewRef?.(fn) in onMount, App stores the function in a let variable and calls it from handleFitToView
Create pickOpenPath following the same structure: import open from @tauri-apps/plugin-dialog, call with filters, normalize return value to string|null
Add onHelp to existing interface, add handler in existing handleKeyDown function. No structural changes needed — just one more case in the if/else chain.
Extend existing createScene mock to return grid/axes objects with visible property. Add mockGrid/mockAxes objects to existing mock setup. Add new test cases within existing describe blocks.
Reuse renderAndWaitForReady for all new App tests. Use capturedViewportProps to verify onHover, evalStatus, fitToViewRef props are passed correctly to Viewport mock.

reuse.what: SolidJS testing patterns (render, screen, fireEvent, waitFor, createRoot)
reuse.how: Follow identical patterns: vi.mock() at module level, beforeEach/afterEach for cleanup, createRoot for hook tests, fireEvent for interaction simulation

reuse.what: Theme CSS variables for consistent component styling
reuse.where: gui/src/theme.ts THEME_TOKENS, existing inline styles in Viewport.tsx and panels
reuse.how: KeyboardHelp overlay uses var(--reify-surface), var(--reify-text), var(--reify-border), var(--reify-accent) consistent with Viewport tooltip, ExportDialog overlay, and other panel components

reuse.what: Splitter drag simulation pattern for App tests
reuse.how: Use same fireEvent.mouseDown/mouseMove/mouseUp pattern with clientX/clientY for testing max-width clamping behavior
